title: ALCOA+ data integrity — control theme 2

compliance.assurance_rationale: Uplifted from contributing to partial: UC provides meaningful monitoring evidence for §11.10(e).
compliance.controlObjective: Evidence that FDA Part 11 §11.10(e) (Audit trails) is enforced — Splunk UC-22.17.17: ALCOA+ data integrity — control theme 2.
compliance.evidenceArtifact: Saved search 'UC-22.17.17' running on LIMS, MES, ELN, CDS, QMS/ERP validation logs, Windows security for GxP workstations, archived to the restricted audit_evidence index (default 7-year retention). Auto-drafted — SME review required.

dataSources: LIMS, MES, ELN, CDS, QMS/ERP validation logs, Windows security for GxP workstations
app: Splunk Enterprise Security (263), Splunk DB Connect (2686), Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Windows (742), Splunk ITSI (1841), Splunk Common Information Model Add-on (1621).
spl:
index=backup sourcetype=commvault:job subclient_name="MES-*" earliest=-7d
| eval uc_tag="22.17.17"
| stats latest(status) as st by subclient_name, client_name
| where st!="Completed"
description: Supports Part 11 technical controls evidence by correlating validated system audit trails with identity and change records.
value: Supports Part 11 technical controls evidence for "ALCOA+ data integrity — control theme 2" by correlating validated system audit trails with identity and change records.
implementation: (1) Confirm field extractions and CIM tags for the sourcetypes in scope; (2) Load or maintain the referenced lookups/KVStore collections with owner attestation dates; (3) Schedule the search at an interval aligned to the control’s materiality; (4) Route positive findings to the compliance ticketing queue with required evidence fields; (5) Retain scheduled search exports per records management and legal hold procedures. Indexes required: `index=backup`.
visualization: Time chart, Table, Single value. Key fields: `client_name`, `st`, `subclient_name`, `uc_tag`.
knownFalsePositives: LIMS audit events during scheduled data corrections, validation testing, or controlled e-signature workflow.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Splunk Enterprise Security (263), Splunk DB Connect (2686), Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Windows (742), Splunk ITSI (1841), Splunk Common Information Model Add-on (1621)..
• Ensure the following data sources are available: LIMS, MES, ELN, CDS, QMS/ERP validation logs, Windows security for GxP workstations.
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
(1) Confirm field extractions and CIM tags for the sourcetypes in scope; (2) Load or maintain the referenced lookups/KVStore collections with owner attestation dates; (3) Schedule the search at an interval aligned to the control’s materiality; (4) Route positive findings to the compliance ticketing queue with required evidence fields; (5) Retain scheduled search exports per records management and legal hold procedures.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=backup sourcetype=commvault:job subclient_name="MES-*" earliest=-7d
| eval uc_tag="22.17.17"
| stats latest(status) as st by subclient_name, client_name
| where st!="Completed"
```

Understanding this SPL

**Commvault MES subclient backups not completed (21 CFR Part 11)** — Supports Part 11 technical controls evidence by correlating validated system audit trails with identity and change records.

Documented **Data sources**: LIMS, MES, ELN, CDS, QMS/ERP validation logs, Windows security for GxP workstations. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Splunk Enterprise Security (263), Splunk DB Connect (2686), Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Windows (742), Splunk ITSI (1841), Splunk Common Information Model Add-on (1621). The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: backup; **sourcetype**: commvault:job. If that sourcetype is not mentioned in Data sources, double-check parsing or update the documentation to match the feed you actually ingest.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=backup, sourcetype=commvault:job, time bounds. Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **uc_tag** — often to normalize units, derive a ratio, or prepare for thresholds.
• `stats` rolls up events into metrics; results are split **by subclient_name, client_name** so each row reflects one combination of those dimensions (useful for per-host, per-user, or per-entity comparisons for this use case).
• Filters the current rows with `where st!="Completed"` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.



Step 3 — Validate
Compare a sample of matches with the source-of-truth system (LIMS, MES, or ELN) and the audit register. Confirm users, instruments, and timestamps line up with controlled quality records for the same activity.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Time chart, Table, Single value
grandmaExplanation: We connect audit trails from lab and plant systems with who did what, so we can show food and drug rules are met for alcoa+ data integrity.
